seo.title: Specialty Testing MA | Ken Arnold Home Inspection
seo.description: Radon testing, well water, termite WDI, mold, thermal imaging, and septic inspections across the Pioneer Valley. Bundle with any home inspection. MA Lic. #587.

sections.heading: Specialty Testing & Inspections
sections.subheading: The Risks a Standard Inspection Can't See
sections.intro: Radon, water quality, wood-destroying insects, hidden moisture, and septic conditions aren't visible on a standard walk-through. Six specialty testing services are available — each independently valuable, all bundleable into a single inspection visit.

paragraphs.html: A standard home inspection evaluates visible, accessible systems and components. It can't detect radioactive gas seeping through the foundation, contaminants dissolved in well water, termite colonies inside wall framing, hidden moisture behind finished surfaces, or a failing septic leach field. These aren't edge cases — they're routine findings across Pioneer Valley properties.

Specialty testing addresses what a visual inspection can't. Each service is independently licensed, independently priced, and independently valuable. Most bundle into the same appointment as a buyer home inspection — one coordinated visit, complete picture, no second trip to the property.

sections.heading: Six Tests.
sections.intro: Every specialty test can be scheduled standalone or added to any inspection appointment. Bundle two or more and save a trip to the property.

cards.title: Radon Testing
cards.body: 48-hour continuous-monitor radon testing in the Pioneer Valley. Add to any home inspection or schedule standalone.

cards.title: Well Water Testing
cards.body: Comprehensive pre-purchase well water testing for rural Pioneer Valley properties. Lab analysis through state-certified labs, results in 5–10 business days.
cards.price_secondary: Bundle with home inspection — package pricing

cards.title: Septic System Inspection
cards.body: Pre-purchase septic system evaluation for rural Pioneer Valley properties. Coordinated with Massachusetts Title 5 inspectors when transfer requires it.

cards.title: Termite & WDI Inspection
cards.body: NPMA-33 wood-destroying insect inspection — required for most VA, FHA, and many investor loans. Carpenter ant, carpenter bee, and powderpost beetle evaluation included.

cards.title: Mold Inspection
cards.body: Visual mold evaluation with moisture-source identification, optional air sampling. Common in older Pioneer Valley basements and mill-conversion units.

cards.title: Thermal Imaging Inspection
cards.body: Infrared scanning surfaces issues invisible to the naked eye — water intrusion, missing insulation, electrical hot spots, and HVAC performance problems.

paragraphs.html: Most specialty services are added to the same appointment as a home inspection — radon monitors are placed during the visit and picked up 48 hours later, water samples are collected on-site, and WDI inspections add 30–45 minutes to on-site time. Bundling keeps property access to one coordinated window and reduces total cost.

checklist.text: Radon testing — monitor placed and retrieved; results within 48 hours
Termite & WDI — 30–45 min add-on; NPMA-33 form delivered promptly
Thermal imaging — standard add-on on most inspections
Mold — visual evaluation + moisture mapping bundled with home inspection
Well water & septic — samples collected on-site during inspection visit
MA License #587 — all testing personally performed by Ken Arnold

items.q: Which specialty tests are most commonly bundled with a home inspection?
items.a: Radon testing is the most common add-on in the Pioneer Valley — the region tests high and the EPA action level is 4.0 pCi/L. Radon testing and termite and WDI inspection together cover the two most common lender-required tests. Thermal imaging is included on most inspections at a bundled rate. For rural properties, well water testing and septic evaluation are both strongly recommended.

items.q: Can I bundle multiple specialty tests into the same appointment?
items.a: Yes — bundling is almost always the right approach. Most specialty tests are added to a home inspection visit, saving a separate trip to the property and reducing total cost. Describe what you're buying when you schedule and we will recommend the right combination.

items.q: Is radon common in the Pioneer Valley?
items.a: Yes. The Pioneer Valley and surrounding Franklin and Hampshire County communities have documented elevated radon levels across much of the region. Geologic factors — granite bedrock, glacial soils, and Connecticut River Valley geology — contribute to above-average radon concentrations. Testing before closing is the only way to know what you're buying.

items.q: Do I need a termite inspection if the house looks fine?
items.a: Termite and WDI damage is often invisible from the exterior. Carpenter ants — by far the most common wood-destroying insect in the Pioneer Valley — typically infest wall cavities and structural framing behind finished surfaces. An NPMA-33 form from a licensed inspector is required for most VA loans and commonly requested on FHA and investor loans.

items.q: Is well water testing required when buying a property with a private well?
items.a: It is required for FHA and VA loans and strongly recommended for all purchases. The Pioneer Valley's geology — particularly in Franklin County and parts of Hampshire County — produces naturally elevated iron, manganese, and in some zones, arsenic and uranium. Our pre-purchase panel covers the full lender suite plus regional-specific contaminants.

items.q: What areas do you serve for specialty testing?
items.a: We perform specialty testing throughout the Pioneer Valley and surrounding communities — including Amherst, Northampton, Greenfield, Deerfield, Hadley, Easthampton, Holyoke, Sunderland, and more. See the service area page for the full list.

sections.heading: Know What You're Buying.
sections.body: Radon, water quality, termites, mold, and septic conditions are all invisible on a standard inspection — and all routine findings across the Pioneer Valley. Specialty testing is available throughout Western Massachusetts, bundled into a single visit or scheduled standalone.
